title: Glorious Golden Welsh Lamb Scotch Egg
description: A romantic British dish featuring lamb and citrus, low in glycemic index, served in a scotch egg form, cooked to a golden brown in an iron teppan.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine lamb mince, demerara sugar, lemon zest, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined.
Divide the lamb mixture into 4 equal parts and shape each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a patty, then wrap each patty around a hard-boiled egg, sealing the edges well.
Dip each scotch egg into the beaten eggs and then roll in breadcrumbs to coat.
Heat the olive oil in the preheated iron teppan and deep-fry the scotch eggs until golden brown, about 4-5 minutes on each side.
Remove the scotch eggs from the oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve the Glorious Golden Welsh Lamb Scotch Eggs hot, garnished with lemon wedges if desired.
